channel.title: Stephanie Kirk | The Clinical Brain Lab

item.title: Stephanie Kirk
item.description: Stephanie earned her B.A. in Psychology from the University of British Columbia (2019) and M.A. in Museum Studies & Curatorial Practices from Nanyang Technological University (2021). She has contributed to several projects which examine the neurocognitive processes underlying belief in deceptive (real versus fake) information, in addition to interdisciplinary work examining the interaction between environment aesthetics, typology, and psychological outcomes using EEG. Presently she is involved in Brain-NORM Project 5 which uses cognitive behavioural measures to examine the relationship between literacy, numeracy and working memory.

item.title: The Heart Can Lie: A Preliminary Investigation of the Role of Interoception and Theory of Mind in Deception
